response.justification: Strong staff-level candidate with exceptional alignment on AI-native environment experience—currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, directly matching the role's baseline expectation of working with agentic systems. Demonstrates required distributed systems expertise (millions of RPS at Dunnhumby) and deep technical breadth across full stack. However, Python is explicitly listed as a must-have skill and is absent from the resume; clarifying Python capability and recent hands-on AI/ML product depth are essential before proceeding.
strengths: Currently at re:cinq as Staff Software Engineer working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators—directly aligned with role's core requirement for comfort with agentic workflow systems

Proven distributed systems and high-throughput architecture expertise: built low-latency systems handling millions of HTTP requests per second with micro-services infrastructure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Staff-level technical leadership across web, backend, infrastructure, and DevOps with demonstrated ownership mindset and ability to learn diverse languages and frameworks
concerns: No Python listed in skills section despite being explicitly required as must-have; unclear whether Python experience exists or represents a true gap
Limited evidence of hands-on ML/LLM product development or model training experience; while nice-to-have, depth here would strengthen credibility for AI infrastructure role
Operational incident-response and on-call rotation experience not explicitly documented; job requires this as must-have non-technical skill

key_factors.factor: Current AI-native environment experience with agentic systems
Distributed systems and high-throughput architecture credentials
